questionText: A playground scoreboard shows a marble rolled 4.7 meters to the nearest tenth and 5 meters to the nearest whole number. Choose the original distance that could be on the measuring tape.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can round decimals to the nearest whole number or tenth
issues: [difficulty_mismatch, distractor_quality] | The item is labeled hard, but solving it only requires checking which option rounds to 4.7 to the nearest tenth and 5 to the nearest whole number; this is closer to medium than hard. | For a hard item, distractors should reflect stronger misconception patterns. Here the options are mostly just nearby decimals, making the challenge mainly answer-checking rather than substantive reasoning. | Fix: Relabel the item as medium, or increase cognitive demand by asking students to generate or justify a range of possible values instead of selecting from options. | Fix: Use distractors tied to specific misconceptions, such as values that satisfy only one rounding condition or boundary values students often mis-handle.
